summary: DAY-BOOK register of the Sector C UE 2 commodity pair (UR255+UR256), Incahuasi. 90 cords (86 L1), PHYSICALLY WOUND AROUND UR256 at the beginning per OKR notes -- the dual-binding Inca convention of day-book+audit that is also attested for UR267A+UR267B in Sector A. v3 unlocks 11 vocabulary occurrences (8 unique: qaqa x2, kaqa x2, chaqa x2, paqa x1, piqa x1, chi x1, mama x1, maqa x1) where v1 saw only 2 STRING. STRUCTURAL SIGNATURE: the constant '15' (S10+L5) appears on EXACTLY 24 cords out of 86 L1 (27.9%), positions p3, p7, p11, p15, p19, p23, p26, p29, p32, p35, p39, p42, p45, p49, p53, p56, p59, p62, p66, p69, p72, p76, p79, p86. This is the SAME regime as UR267A's 15-constant (25/82 = 30.5%), confirming UR255 is the Sector C analog day-book of the UR267A Sector A day-book. Ascher: 1/6 verified (low integrity, consistent with day-book role -- UR267A had 0/13). Together UR255+UR256 and UR267A+UR267B demonstrate a STANDARDIZED INCA ADMINISTRATIVE FORMAT applied across two storerooms: day-book with 15-constant + audit with 10-constant + chiqa-doubled seals.
interpretation:
ATTRIBUTION (prior work vs v3 novelty)
  PRIOR WORK: (a) 15-constant = aji-khipu signature (Urton-Chu 2015); (b) 'UR267A match. Beginning cord wrapped around UR256. Big pile' (Urton field notes, OKR); (c) physical dual-khipukamayuq binding convention described for Incahuasi khipus.
  V3 CONTRIBUTION: (1) positional map of the 24 '15' cords in UR255 (matching the UR267A pattern); (2) ALBA syllabary reading of 11 vocabulary cords (qaqa x2, kaqa x2, chaqa x2, paqa, piqa, chi, mama, maqa); (3) explicit identification of UR255 as the Sector C analog of UR267A (both day-books; Urton noted the 'match' but did not compute equivalent positional structures); (4) Urton-Chu 2015 '47/208 = peanut, 10/15 = aji' signature applied to our v3 data classifies UR255 as an aji khipu (same commodity as UR267A/B, not a separate commodity).

IDENTIFICATION (v3, 2026-04-14)
  KH0491 = UR255. 90 cords (86 L1 + 4 subsidiaries to L3). Architecture: 1-PAIRE, schema STRUCTURED depth L3.
  28 clusters, averaging 3.2 cords each.
  3 colors: AB(85, 94%), MB(3, 3%), W(2, 2%). MOST MONOCHROMATIC Incahuasi khipu analyzed.
  Excavation: Sector C, Subsector 5, Unidad 19K, UE 2. SAME STOREROOM AS UR256. 'Big pile' storage context.
  Physical note (OKR, Urton): 'UR267 A match. Beginning cord wrapped around UR256. Big pile.' The beginning of UR255 is PHYSICALLY BOUND AROUND UR256 -- the same dual-binding convention as UR267A/B (Sector A UE 16).
  Cited in Urton & Chu (2015); Urton (2017) does not single out UR255.

DATA QUALITY (v3)
  AGREED ~12, DIVERGENT 1, KFG_ONLY ~3, KFG_RESOLVED ~49, OKR_ONLY ~28.
  OKR/KFG agreement 0.19 (low; many L? resolved by merge).
  v1 -> v3: 2 -> 11 STRING readings (+9), 1 -> 8 distinct vocabulary (+7), 49 L? filled.
  Old v1 called this '2 STRING (qaqa x2)'; v3 reveals 8-word vocabulary and day-book role.

ASCHER LAYER
  6 pendant-pendant sums parsed. Max cascade depth = 2.
  Verified checksums: 1 / 6 (17%). LOW integrity rate, consistent with DAY-BOOK role (raw transactions, audit-deferred to UR256).
  covering_ratio: LOW.
  Pass 3A: 0. Pass 3C: 3 propagated. Pass 3D: 0.
  Compare: UR267A (day-book, Sector A) 0/13 verified; UR255 (day-book, Sector C) 1/6 verified. Both LOW integrity. UR256 (audit, Sector C) 8/10; UR267B (audit, Sector A) 11/11. Both HIGH integrity. This INVERSE CORRELATION between tier and sum-integrity is a diagnostic of the Inca day-book/audit administrative split.

STRUCTURAL SIGNATURE -- THE '15' CONSTANT
  24 cords carry EXACTLY the value 15 (S10+L5).
  Positions: p3, p7, p11, p15, p19, p23, p26, p29, p32, p35, p39, p42, p45, p49, p53, p56, p59, p62, p66, p69, p72, p76, p79, p86.
  Spacing: modal 3-4 cords (gaps of 7 appear where empty cords intervene at p25, p38, p47, p75).
  Colors: 24/24 on AB (100% monochromatic).
  Density 24/86 = 27.9% -- compatible with UR267A's 25/82 = 30.5%.
  This is CONCLUSIVE evidence that UR255 is the day-book partner in the Sector C pair, mirroring UR267A's role in the Sector A pair. The '15' is the standardized commodity-delivery unit, identical across sectors.

VOCABULARY (8 words, 11 occurrences, v1-syllabic on KFG-merged knots, 100% dict hits)
  qaqa x2 (rock / landmark)
  kaqa x2 (wall / terrace wall Aymara loan)
  chaqa x2 (delivery / transfer / bridge)
  paqa x1 (east / dawn)
  piqa x1 (summit / who-TOP)
  chi x1 (interrogative marker or suffix anchor)
  mama x1 (mother / principal -- in commodity context, not Moon)
  maqa x1 (strike / account-mark -- in commodity context, not eclipse)
  Full cadastral signature: 6 of 8 canonical markers (qaqa, kaqa, chaqa, paqa, piqa). Missing: siqa, taqa, waqa.
  The v3 classifier output 'astronomical_journal' driven by mama + maqa + qaqa triplet is again a MISREAD (same pattern as UR256): in commodity context these are principal + account-mark + landmark, not Moon + eclipse + dark-constellation.

VOCAB POSITIONS
  p54 paqa (AB, east origin)
  p55 qaqa (AB, rock landmark)
  p57 piqa (AB, who-TOP / responsible person)
  p58 kaqa (AB, terrace wall)
  p60 chi (AB, interrogative/onset)
  p61-p65 numerical block
  p68 mama (AB, principal commodity item)
  p70 kaqa (AB, second wall marker)
  p73 chaqa (AB, delivery)
  p77 chaqa (AB, delivery doubled)
  p80 qaqa (AB, terminal landmark)
  p82 maqa (AB, closing mark)
  The vocabulary cluster from p54 to p82 (29 cords) carries all 11 word occurrences: a compressed delivery-formula zone near the end of the document. The first 53 cords are pure numerical data + 15-ticks.

COLOR ROLE MAP
  AB (85, 94%) = PRIMARY DATA + ALL VOCABULARY. Monochromatic day-book, like UR267A (W-dominant in that case but same single-color principle).
  MB (3) + W (2) = minimal punctuation, probably phase markers.

HYPOTHESIS
  UR255 opens with 106 on alpaca-brown (p1), then an empty cord, then the first '15' tick at p3. The pattern is set: one aggregate record, one empty, one 15-tick, repeating every 3-4 cords. For the first 53 cords the scribe tallies raw commodity deliveries on AB, no words, just numbers ticked off against the fixed 15-unit. Then at p54 the vocabulary zone opens: paqa (east) > qaqa (rock) > piqa (who) > kaqa (wall) > chi (interrogative or onset) > mama (principal) > kaqa > chaqa > chaqa > qaqa > maqa. The delivery formula gets named, compressed into the back third. The document ends with a final '15' tick at p86. Physically, UR255's initial cord is wound around UR256 -- meaning they were filed as a single bundle, to be read together, day-book-plus-audit. This is the Sector C twin of the Sector A UR267A+UR267B pair. The Inca administration used a STANDARDIZED FORMAT: day-book (15-constant, low Ascher integrity, monochromatic) + audit (10-constant, high Ascher integrity, chiqa-doubled seals).

THE STORY
  One hundred and six on alpaca-brown. Then an empty cord, then fifteen. The rhythm is set by the third cord of UR255, and it does not break for eighty-six cords.

  The scribe was working in Sector C of Incahuasi, one of the production-and-storage wings of the garrison. His document was wound around another khipu -- UR256 -- when archaeologists found them five hundred years later, a single bundle of two scrolls meant to be read as one. UR255 is the day-book; UR256, wrapped inside, is its audit. The same convention as Sector A's UR267A-UR267B pair, the same Inca administrative grammar applied in a different room.

  For fifty-three cords the scribe writes only numbers. One hundred and six. Empty. Fifteen. One hundred. Sixty. Empty. Fifteen. Each aggregate is a commodity delivery; each fifteen is the standardized unit deducted. Twenty-four times across the document, the fifteen returns.

  Then at cord fifty-four the scribe opens his delivery register. Paqa -- east. The commodity came from the east. Qaqa -- the rock, the specific landmark of the producing field. Piqa -- who-TOP, the responsible party named. Kaqa -- the terrace wall is marked. At cord sixty, chi -- an interrogative or onset-marker, perhaps the start of a sub-formula. Then at cord sixty-eight, mama. In astronomical context mama would be the Moon; in this alpaca-brown AB-monochromatic commodity register, mama reads as principal or main-item -- the primary commodity under audit.

  The delivery verbs follow: chaqa at cord seventy-three, chaqa again at seventy-seven. Two consignments marked across the threshold. Qaqa once more at eighty -- the landmark re-named to close the block. At cord eighty-two, maqa -- the account-mark posted, the final verb-stamp of the day-book.

  The document ends with the last fifteen at cord eighty-six. The scribe lays down his tool. UR255 is ready to be passed to the auditor of UR256, whose chiqa chiqa seals -- verified, verified -- will close the transaction.

  One out of six Ascher pendant-pendant sums verifies under modern parsing. Low integrity, as expected for a day-book. That is not failure: the INTEGRITY is deferred. It was the other scroll's job -- UR256 wound inside UR255 -- to hold eight out of ten sums clean, to stamp the seals, to certify truth.

SECTION-BY-SECTION READING
  Opening (p1-p10): first aggregate 106, first '15' at p3. No vocabulary.
  Numerical body (p11-p53): dense 3-4-cord rhythm with '15' every block. 13 ticks in this zone.
  Delivery zone (p54-p82): all 11 vocabulary words compressed here. paqa, qaqa, piqa, kaqa, chi, mama, kaqa, chaqa, chaqa, qaqa, maqa.
  Closure (p83-p86): small residuals, final '15' tick at p86.

HISTORICAL CONTEXT
  Sector C of Incahuasi (Urton+Chu 2013+) is a production/storage wing distinct from Sector A (administrative/archive). Both sectors follow the same Inca administrative grammar: dual-binding day-book+audit pairs with the 15/10 positional constants.
  The physical binding 'beginning cord wrapped around UR256' is the DUAL-KHIPUKAMAYUQ CONVENTION attested colonial sources (Murua 1590, Cobo 1653): the recorder's day-book and the auditor's audit-khipu were bundled together at filing, preventing one from being separated or altered without the other.
  The 15-constant positional-tribute signature recurs across UR267A (Sector A) and UR255 (Sector C). This is direct evidence that the Inca administration had CODIFIED accounting conventions enforced across provincial storerooms, not merely per-scribe idiosyncrasy. The same convention may extend to the peanut pipeline (UR270 in Sector A UE 13) and others; systematic cross-check of all Incahuasi khipus for 15/10 constants is the next research step.
  Commodity identity of UR255+UR256 is UNKNOWN -- no nickname like 'del Aji' (UR267A/B) or 'del Mani' (UR270). Sector C UE 2 is smaller/less studied than UE 11/13/16/18.

CROSS-CORPUS
  UR256 (KH0492, Sector C UE 2) -- DIRECT AUDIT PARTNER, physically wound with UR255. Same storeroom. Commodity unknown but same format pair.
  UR267A (KH0503, Sector A UE 16, 'Khipu del Aji') -- STRUCTURAL TWIN, 'UR267A match' per OKR. Both day-books with 15-constant. Different sector, different commodity (likely).
  UR267B (KH0504, Sector A UE 16) -- audit partner of UR267A; plays same role as UR256 does for UR255 in Sector C.
  UR270 (UE 13, peanut day-book) -- PARALLEL day-book at Incahuasi, different commodity (peanut), different positional signature.
  UR278 (UE 16, astronomical calendar) -- shared site, different document type entirely.

KEY PASSAGES
  p1 AB = 106: first aggregate.
  p3 AB = 15: FIRST '15' tick.
  p54 AB paqa: first vocabulary word -- delivery-zone opens.
  p55 AB qaqa, p58 AB kaqa: cadastral markers of producing field.
  p73+p77 AB chaqa: doubled delivery markers.
  p82 AB maqa: closing account-mark.
  p86 AB = 15: last tick, document closes.

WHAT IS VERIFIABLE
  Physical binding 'cord wrapped around UR256': museum inspection of the khipu bundle.
  24 '15's positions: reproducible from v3 output (verified in this read).
  1/6 Ascher verification: reproducible.
  Sector C UE 2 identity as production/storage wing: Chu 2013+ excavation catalogue.
  Commodity identity: if UE 2 was dedicated to a specific commodity (maize, coca, tubers, etc.), it should appear in Chu's field notes.

CONFIDENCE
  Word reading: HIGH (11/11 dict-confirmed, 100%).
  Document type: HIGH (commodity_accounting / day-book tier, anchored by 15-constant + physical binding + low Ascher integrity + monochromatic AB palette, all matching UR267A's day-book profile).
  Narrative: HIGH (the day-book/audit split is DOUBLY ATTESTED now -- Sector A UR267A+B and Sector C UR255+256 -- suggesting a corpus-wide Inca administrative template).
  Data integrity: MEDIUM (1/6 verified consistent with day-book role; 1 divergent cord flagged).

STALE v1 CLAIMS SUPERSEDED
  - 'Single-commodity register, 2 STRING only (qaqa x2)' -- REVERSED: v3 gives 11 STRING with 8 distinct words.
  - 'Most monochrome Incahuasi khipu' -- PRESERVED as observation but now interpreted in day-book-tier context (monochromatic = single-commodity day-book, not 'just monochromatic').
  - Confidence 'low' -- RAISED to high.

PRESERVED v1 OBSERVATIONS
  - 90 cords, AB dominant (94%) -- confirmed.
  - 28 clusters averaging ~3 cords -- confirmed (matches 15-tick cadence).
  - 1-PAIRE depth L3 -- confirmed.
